insight: Blood Pact is best used as a last resort when the alternative is holding Drones, or as tempo defense against burst damage, or to punish breachproof strategies by killing the Grimbotch. Casually buying it just for convenient defense is discouraged.
author: redrame
context: Community discussion advising a newer player on when Blood Pact is and isn't appropriate to buy.

insight: Blood Pact is significantly better than holding 3 Drones defensively; if one player buys Blood Pact while the other holds Drones, the Drone-holder will be at a substantial disadvantage.
author: redrame
context: Discussion of Blood Pact's relative defensive value compared to holding Drones during a game review.

insight: Blood Pact's granularity can make it worth buying earlier than usual if it prevents your opponent from exploiting your attack count; in such cases you are effectively paying for more than 4 defense. Husk is preferable if you simply need granular defense without the other Blood Pact considerations.
author: redrame
context: Detailed breakdown of Blood Pact's niche advantages during a teaching discussion.

insight: Blood Pact gains additional value in sets with chill because chill generates extra value against the Grimbotch that Blood Pact produces.
author: apooche
context: apooche adding a nuance to the Blood Pact discussion about chill synergy.

insight: Blood Pact punishes breachproof strategies because the opponent can simply kill the Grimbotch, making breachproofing generally inadvisable in Blood Pact sets unless the set is so obviously breachproof-favored that you can account for it.
author: redrame
context: Explaining Blood Pact's anti-breachproof function during a teaching conversation.

insight: Blood Pact also punishes synced Scorchillas or Iso Kronuses, in addition to breachproof strategies.
author: mrguy888
context: mrguy888 adding additional cases where Blood Pact provides strategic counterplay.

insight: Doomed Mech should generally be used for its absorb (defending) rather than always clicking for attack, especially when you are also buying Blood Pact. Holding Doomed Mech to absorb for 4 lets you spend less gold on defense and more on attack, and is even more valuable when both players are buying Blood Pacts.
author: redrame
context: Replay review of a Blood Pact-heavy game where the player was clicking Doomed Mech for attack every turn.

insight: The more extreme the defensive situation becomes, the more valuable holding back Doomed Mech for absorb is, unless it is actively breaching.
author: apooche
context: apooche reinforcing redrame's advice about Doomed Mech usage in high-pressure defensive situations.

insight: Thermite Core is considered the clearest example of a unit that is mechanically functional (converting attack into Pixies) but whose function is rarely strategically useful, making it the closest thing to a unit a player should generally avoid building around.
author: redrame
context: Discussion about whether any units are categorically bad, with thermite core offered as the best example of a near-useless unit.

insight: Small units are generally meant to support larger ones rather than form the core of a strategy on their own. For example, when Immolite and Tantalum Ray are both available, there is not two strategies — there is a Tantalum Ray strategy that Immolite supports.
author: redrame
context: General strategic advice being given to a newer player about how to read a set.

insight: Unit power ranking lists are considered problematic for Prismata because unit value is highly context-dependent on the set; ranking units like Corpus above Immolite in a vacuum gives no meaningful strategic insight about any specific game.
author: redrame
context: Expert discussion about the usefulness and limitations of community-made unit tier lists.
